# Paper 3 — per-claim evidence graph (schema v2)

One row per registry gate wiring (a gate may split into several claim links). `evidence_strength` is the **per-gate adjudicated** tier; `component_evidence_strength` is the component's own declared tier (context only). `relationship` is the honest fit/evaluation relationship. `NEEDS_ADJUDICATION` rows are drafts — their verdicts are deliberately blank until curated.

**54 links total — 54 adjudicated, 0 awaiting adjudication.**

## Adjudicated claims

| link | owner | p3 use | support | tier | relationship | observable | claim |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `brewer2026.coupled_kappa_t::gate_kappa_t_composition_diagnostic` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | exploratory_synthesis | post_fit_same_data | Minimum composite porosity (eps_min vs eps0) and the 9-bar Q(t) RMSE vs the measured Waszkiewicz trace (g/s). | Adding the parameter-free mo2023_2 swelling branch behaves as the framework predicts: the composite porosity closes below eps0 and the 9-bar Q(t) residual jumps from ~0.12 to ~0.65 g/s (worse than the flat null ~0.603), diagnosing that mo2023_2's fixed-dP fresh-grain swelling is mis-scaled for an already-swollen saturated pre-wet rig. |
| `brewer2026.coupled_kappa_t::gate_kappa_t_degeneracy::reconstruction` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | Mass flow Q(t) at 9 bar vs the measured Waszkiewicz trace over the 15-95 s saturated window (RMSE, g/s). | In that extraction-only reduction the coupled model's predicted 9-bar Q(t) reproduces the measured Waszkiewicz 9-bar flow ramp with RMSE ~0.113 g/s (gate <0.13), matching the poroelastic component's own reconstruction (rung 4). |
| `brewer2026.coupled_kappa_t::gate_kappa_t_degeneracy::reduction` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | Structural identity of the coupled model's extraction-only Phi(t)/Q(t) closure to the standalone poroelastic component (a code property). | With the swelling/compaction/fines-loss branches disabled, the coupled shared-porosity synthesis reduces exactly to its degenerate special case waszkiewicz2025.poroelastic (extraction-only Phi(t)=m_d(t)/m0 through the same poroelastic closure), adding no new behaviour in this limit. |
| `brewer2026.lb_reference::gate_lb_channel` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | Darcy permeability k of a plane-Poiseuille channel in lattice units vs the closed-form analytic value h^2/12. | The D3Q19 TRT lattice-Boltzmann permeability kernel reproduces the exact plane-Poiseuille channel permeability h^2/12 to within 0.5% (reported +0.03%). |
| `brewer2026.pack_generator::gate_pack_generator_admissible` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | qualitative_capacity | not_empirical | Generated solid fraction vs target, grain radius in voxels, and heterogeneity-field mean/std. | The overlapping-sphere pack generator hits its target solid fraction (0.55 -> 0.551), produces an admissible boolean pack at resolution (grain radius >= 10 voxels), and its columnar heterogeneity field is zero-mean / unit-std. |
| `brewer2026.streamtube::gate_streamtube_heldout` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | within_campaign_held_out | within_campaign_held_out | Held-out relative EY deviation (measured yield below the homogeneous-flow model) at the held-out grind. | The sigma(GS) fines closure, fit on two of Cameron's three Fig-5 grinds, predicts the held-out grind's relative EY deviation to <2 pp (leave-one-out over GS 1.1/1.3/1.5; observed max held-out error ~1.1 pp). |
| `cameron2020.extraction_bdf::gate_cameron_conservation` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | Extraction yield EY (%) via two internal accounting routes plus an inventory upper bound, all computed from the simulation and module constants. | The cameron2020 BDF two-population solver conserves solute mass (cup-mass EY agrees with solid-depletion EY to within 0.5 pp) and the predicted EY stays strictly below the model's own per-bed soluble-inventory ceiling. |
| `fasano2000_partI.fines_migration::gate_fasano_cor82_nonmonotone` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | sign_or_compatibility | code_verification | Location of the q_inf(p0) peak vs the steepest-slope knee of the beta(q) threshold, plus non-monotonicity of q_inf(p0). | In Fasano's digitized figures the peak of the asymptotic-discharge curve q_inf(p0) (Fig 8.6) sits at the flow where the detachment threshold beta(q) (Fig 8.7) drops steeply, the structural link the paper's Corollary 8.2 requires. |
| `fasano2000_partI.fines_migration::gate_fasano_freeboundary` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | Discharge trace q(t), free-boundary position s(t), global fines mass balance, and q_inf vs injection pressure p0. | A free-boundary fines-migration solver with puckworks' own (invented) closures exhibits Fasano's analytic structure: global mass balance <1%, q(t) monotone non-increasing, s(t)>= s_min, and a non-monotone q_inf(p0) with an interior peak. |
| `fasano2000_partI.fines_migration::gate_fasano_reversal_signature` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | same_campaign_not_held_out | Peak discharge rate (mL/s) within each of the three experimental segments (direct / off-on resume / inverted). | In the digitized Fig 8.4 reversible-chamber experiment the direct segment and the chamber-inverted segment both replay a large transient discharge peak while the pressure-off/on resume segment stays at the low plateau — the fines-counter-migration reversal signature. |
| `foster2025.infiltration::gate_infiltration_triangle` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | sign_or_compatibility | same_campaign_not_held_out | First-liquid / bed-saturation time (s): observed = time cup weight first exceeds 0.5 g; predicted = t_saturate when the modelled front reaches bed depth L, at porosity endpoints 0.173 and 0.322. | A sharp-front infiltration prediction, driven by the DE1 shot's own measured pressure trace and a permeability from that shot's fitted kappa, yields a bed-saturation-time window (across an a-priori porosity bracket 0.173-0.322) that brackets the observed first-drip time (~7 s). |
| `foster2025.machine_mode::gate_foster_ct_trajectory` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | same_campaign_not_held_out | Wetting-front depth s(t) and headspace height H(t) (mm) over the shot, vs the paper's fitted ODE curves and vs digitized CT measurements. | The machine-mode solver's front s(t) and headspace H(t) match Foster's own fitted ODE curves to <0.2 mm RMSE and each bracket at least 4 digitized CT data points within their (>=0.5 mm) error bars. |
| `foster2025.machine_mode::gate_foster_fig15_flowmin` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | code_verification | Normalized bed flow Q/Qm = min(Q_p, f(H,s))/Q_m vs experiment time. | The re-implemented Foster machine-mode ODE (pump characteristic + trapped-air headspace) reproduces the paper's own Fig 15 normalized bed-flow curve, dipping to Q/Qm~0.181 at t~2.0 s and recovering, to RMSE <0.01 over the t_p..t_s window. |
| `foster2025.machine_mode::gate_foster_machine_tp_ts` | paper3 | cited_science | admissible | source_curve_reproduction | post_fit_same_data | Two scalar times (s): ponding time t_p and saturation time t_s, model-computed vs the authors' reported values. | The staged pump/headspace machine-mode ODE, run from the transcribed Table I/II parameters and offset by the fitted t_shift=0.796 s, reproduces the authors' reported ponding time t_p=0.823 s and saturation time t_s=6.669 s. |
| `grudeva2025.reduced::gate_grudeva_no_eps_kappa` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | The algebraic form of the capacitance coefficient B, and kappa from Eq 6.14 at nominal card inputs. | The reduced solver's capacitance coefficient B equals phi_l/phi_T + phi_f/phi_T with NO epsilon factor (the adjudicated no-eps form), and recomputing Eq 6.14 at 9.2 bar yields the adjudicated kappa ~2.2e-15 m^2 rather than the printed 2.2e-16 (decade-typo correction). |
| `grudeva2025.reduced::gate_grudeva_reduced_solver` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | Per-vial solubles mass (g) for vials 3-15, total solubles mass, and existence of the saturated exit plateau. | The reduced solve produces a post-first-drip saturated exit plateau and reconstructs the C1 per-vial solubles masses — total solute within 5% of the 14-shot mean and >=8 of 13 vials within 1 SD. |
| `lee2023.feedback::gate_lee_feedback_negative_result` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | qualitative_capacity | not_empirical | Sign of pathway EY divergence, qualitative shape of EY vs grind (peak+decline vs plateau) at two rho_c, and two nondimensional tau_shot values. | The two-pathway extraction->porosity->permeability model reproduces lee2023's documented sign/shape behaviour — seed amplification, an interior EY(g) peak with fine-side decline ONLY at the imposed unphysical rho_c=798, a plateau at physical rho_c=399, and the reviewer's tau_shot cross-check values (1.12, 0.48). |
| `liang2021.desorption::gate_liang_eoven_ceiling` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | source_curve_reproduction | within_campaign_held_out | E_oven (%) and equilibrium E (%) vs brew ratio R_brew (>=3) from Fig 4, plus the inequality K*E_max < cameron_inventory_ceiling. | The Eq-22 oven-drying kernel reproduces the Fig 4 oven branch (MAPE<0.15), the equilibrium E is flat at ~21%, and the immersion equilibrium ceiling K*E_max (0.215) sits below cameron's per-bed soluble-inventory ceiling. |
| `liang2021.desorption::gate_liang_kemax_refit` | paper3 | cited_science | admissible | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | The dimensionless equilibrium desorption product K*E_max from equilibrium TDS vs brew ratio R_brew for full-immersion brewing. | Re-fitting the lumped equilibrium product K*E_max via Eq. 11 to the digitized Fig. 3 TDS-vs-brew-ratio data (R_brew>=3) recovers ~0.215, the card's reported value. |
| `mo2023_2.coupled_bed::gate_mo2_coupled_bed_fig8` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | Yield (%) vs beverage mass M_c (g), type-M, q=2/3/4 mL/s, M_c<30 g; within-bars count, shape spread, mass-balance floor. | The depth-resolved coupled through-flow bed reproduces mo2023_2's type-M fixed-flow yield-vs-beverage-mass (Fig 8, M_c<30 g) within the digitized replicate error bars at 5/9 points and with tighter shape-spread than the reduced lumped bed, while staying mass-conserving. |
| `mo2023_2.swelling::gate_mo2_fixed_flow_trends` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | sign_or_compatibility | same_campaign_not_held_out | Mean yield (%) over the M_c 18-32 g window, across q (2 vs 4 mL/s) and across powders E/M/F at matched q. | The digitized mo2023_2 fixed-flow yield data exhibit the paper's stated qualitative trends: slower flow gives higher yield at matched beverage mass, and finer grind (E>M>F) gives higher yield, monotone with no fine-grind dip. |
| `mo2023_2.swelling::gate_mo2_k0_carman_kozeny` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | Per-powder relative error between the recomputed CK k0 and the tabulated Table 2 k0 (m^2). | The closed-form Carman-Kozeny k0 with eps_b=0.17, n=0.5 reproduces mo2023_2 Table 2 k0 from the Table 1 Sauter diameter for all four powders to <3%. |
| `mo2023_2.swelling::gate_mo2_swelling_flow_decay` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | code_verification | Fixed-dp flow-decay ratio q(60s)/q(0) (dp/mu/L cancelled) per powder E/H/M/F, plus the scalar s_m. | The re-implemented swelling-PDE -> Eq.21 bed-porosity -> Carman-Kozeny chain reproduces the paper's SIMULATED Fig 3(a) fixed-dp flow-decay ratio q(60s)/q(0) for all four powders within 20%, preserves the coarser-throttles-less ordering E<H<M<F, and matches the closed-form max swelling s_m=3.57%. |
| `mo2023_2.swelling::gate_mo2_swelling_insensitivity` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | End-of-shot yield (fraction of extractable inventory) with swelling on vs off at q=2/3/4 mL/s (fixed-q), contrasted with the fixed-dP Carman-Kozeny flow-decay ratio. | At fixed flow the implemented mo2023_2 two-population model gives nearly identical yield with swelling on vs off (max relative difference <5%) because swelling raises grain porosity/D_p (~+15%) but grows R^2 (~+7%) and these offset — reproducing the paper's Fig. 2 model behaviour. |
| `moroney2016.surrogate::gate_moroney2015_kappa_anchors` | paper3 | resource_context | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | post_fit_same_data | Predicted-vs-measured cylindrical Delta_p (Pa) at the two permeability anchors. | moroney2015's Kozeny-Carman closure (k_h = k_sv1^2 phi_h^3 / (36 kappa (1-phi_h)^2)) with the measured kappa=3.1 and the Table-2 compacted phi_h reproduces the measured cylindrical Delta_p at Q=250 mL/min for both grinds (JK, Cimbali #20) within ~5%. |
| `moroney2016.surrogate::gate_moroney_fig6_washthrough` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | post_fit_same_data | Nondimensional exit concentration c_h vs time: model half-max time vs data t at c=0.5, and early-region RMSE. | The leading-order asymptotic composite exit-concentration solution reproduces Fig 6's saturated plateau for t<1 and the wash-through timing (c=1/2 near the data midpoint) with early-region RMSE<0.15 — qualitatively. |
| `pannusch2024.closures::gate_pannusch_closures` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | Scalar closure outputs at fixed inputs: mu(363K), rho(363K), D per solute, K(Tref)/K80/K98 ratios, and h_sl at two flow rates. | The pannusch2024 constitutive closures reproduce physical anchors: water viscosity ~3.15e-4 Pa.s and density ~962 kg/m^3 at 90C, Wilke-Chang D in 5e-9..1.2e-8 m^2/s, van't Hoff K(Tref)=Kref with weak T-dependence, and a positive Sherwood h_sl monotone in flow. |
| `pannusch2024.solver::gate_pannusch_solver_mape` | paper3 | cited_science | admissible | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | Per-solute MAPE (%) between simulated and measured fraction concentrations over 15 experiments x 6 time-fraction windows. | Run with the authors' Table 2 per-solute fitted parameters, the two-grain multi-solute PDE solver reproduces the authors' own published fit MAPEs against the Schmieder kinetics (TDS 6.07, caffeine 4.59, trigonelline 7.85, CGA 4.98 %, each within ~3.5 pts). |
| `romancorrochano2017.extraction::gate_roman_bed_flow_trend` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | Bed mass-balance residual and yield monotonicity over 40 s, plus ordering of yield and strength at fixed beverage volume for Q=0.8/1.5/3.0e-6 m^3/s. | The lumped flow-through bed solver (Eq 3.36) is mass-conserving (<2%) with monotone yield, and at a matched beverage volume a lower flow Q yields both higher extraction and higher strength than a higher Q. |
| `romancorrochano2017.extraction::gate_roman_bed_mpe_parameter_free` | paper3 | cited_science | admissible | source_curve_reproduction | same_campaign_not_held_out | Mean percentage error (MPE, %) between the thesis's predicted and measured espresso yields per molecular-weight Deff class, digitized from Fig. 7.4 (~+/-0.5 pp). | Across all 15 digitized Fig. 7.4 espresso conditions the thesis's reported medium-MW (non-fitted microstructural Deff) bed-scale prediction error is MPE <=14.3%, and the medium-MW single-Deff column has a lower mean MPE than the low- or high-MW columns. |
| `romancorrochano2017.extraction::gate_roman_mw_temperature_trends` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | same_campaign_not_held_out | Deff class ordering (Table 4.9); K(20/50/80C) magnitude/monotonicity vs Table 4.10; t50 ordering by Deff and equilibrium-extent vs K in the stirred-vessel solver. | Driven by the thesis's own parameter tables, the model's Deff spectrum orders low>med>high>vhigh (Table 4.9), the Arrhenius K(T) reproduces Table 4.10 to <0.05 and rises with T, and a higher-Deff species reaches 50% extraction faster with equilibrium rising in K. |
| `romancorrochano2017.extraction::gate_roman_sphere_solver` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | Max abs difference between the numerical MOL sphere release (N=120) and the analytic Crank series at t=0.5..40 s. | The spherical method-of-lines diffusion solver reproduces the Crank analytic fractional-release series (sphere into an infinite sink) to max abs error <1e-3 across seven times spanning the shot. |
| `romancorrochano2017.extraction::gate_roman_tamped_kappa` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | sign_or_compatibility | same_campaign_not_held_out | Band membership of the 12 measured kappa values and the grind-ordering monotonicity across three densities. | The thesis's measured tamped-bed Darcy permeabilities (Table 6.1) all sit in the 1e-14..1e-12 m^2 literature band (max >1e-13) and, at every initial bed density, a coarser grind gives higher kappa. |
| `romancorrochano2017.extraction::gate_roman_y0_ceiling_sizeexclusion` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | sign_or_compatibility | same_campaign_not_held_out | Ordering of y0 vs the cameron and liang code-constant ceilings, and monotone decrease of the transcribed y0(grind) across six grinds. | The thesis's extractable-inventory ceiling y0=31.7% sits above Cameron's per-bed EY ceiling (24.5%) which sits above Liang's single-immersion equilibrium ceiling (21.5%), and y0 decreases monotonically along the coarsening grind ladder (size-exclusion signal). |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_foursource_spread` | paper3 | resource_context | context_only | sign_or_compatibility | independent_external | Per-cell percent offset (mu_sobolik - mu_TR2001)/mu_TR2001 and (mu_khomyakov - mu_TR2001)/mu_TR2001 over the overlap, summarized by sign and median. | sobolik2002 (Eq-5 dilute closure) joins khomyakov2020 as a third independent liquor-viscosity source; BOTH run systematically ABOVE the telisromero2001 Eq-10 closure across the Xw 76-90 %% / T 30-80 C overlap (sobolik median ~+54%%), so TR2001 is the probable LOW outlier (3-vs-1). |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_intersource_spread` | paper3 | resource_context | context_only | sign_or_compatibility | independent_external | Per-cell percent offset (mu_khomyakov - mu_TR2001)/mu_TR2001 over the overlapping concentration/temperature cells, summarized by median/min/max and sign. | Two independent measured coffee-extract viscosity datasets (khomyakov2020 kinematic converted to dynamic mu=rho*nu vs telisromero2001 Eq-10 closure) agree in sign/direction across their Xw 76-90% / T 295-365 K overlap but khomyakov runs systematically ~+37% (median 25-50%, max <60%) above TR2001. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_mu_bias_directional` | paper3 | resource_context | context_only | sign_or_compatibility | not_empirical | Early-shot flow factor (mu_water/mu_espresso) and late/equilibrium flow factor under an algebraic Darcy Q~1/mu relation. | Substituting the reference espresso viscosity (~1.3-2x pure water) into a Darcy Q~1/mu relation suppresses early-shot flow to ~0.5-0.8x the pure-water prediction while leaving the dilute late/equilibrium flow unchanged. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_reference_mu_above_water` | paper3 | resource_context | context_only | sign_or_compatibility | not_empirical | Reference espresso dynamic-viscosity ratio to pure water at brew temperature, plus Newtonian-band membership. | The transcribed reference espresso viscosity estimate (~4-6e-4 Pa.s, ~1.3-1.9x pure water 3.15e-4 Pa.s) exceeds pure water, stays within 1.0-2.5x, and lies in the Newtonian band (espresso 4-12% solids is below the ~36%-solids power-law onset). |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_sobolik_closures` | paper3 | resource_context | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | post_fit_same_data | Spot-value comparison of the computed Eq (4)/(5)/(11) lines against the authors' own stated values. | The sobolik2002 Eq (4)/(5) viscosity and Eq (11) conductivity COMPUTED lines reproduce the paper's own stated spot values (mu(omega=0.5,60C)~=0.019 Pa.s; mu(omega=0,20C)~=water; electrical conductivity peaks ~3.2 S/m near omega~0.4, 70 C) -- a transcription check of the digitized/computed closures of a concentrated soluble-coffee extract. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_telisromero2000_thermal` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | post_fit_same_data | Density rho, specific heat cp, thermal conductivity k and thermal diffusivity alpha of soluble-coffee extract vs T and water fraction (plus a unit guard). | The transcribed Telis-Romero 2000 linear thermophysical closures (rho/cp/k/alpha) reproduce the card's figure-endpoint / water-limit anchors within plotting accuracy, the typo-corrected Eq(6) alpha coefficient reproduces the Fig-5 endpoint while the printed one does not, and the fraction-vs-percent guard raises on percent input. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_telisromero_closure` | paper3 | resource_context | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | post_fit_same_data | Predicted eta at the Table-1 anchor cell and K at the Table-2 anchor cell (relative error vs the source's measured values), plus the bulk-shot mu/water ratio. | The transcribed Telis-Romero 2001 Eq-10 (eta) and Eq-13 (K) closures reproduce the card's two quoted measured anchor points within the authors' stated fit error (eta<=3%, K<=10%), and the Eq-10 bulk-shot-TDS viscosity is ~1.06x pure water. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_telisromero_full_table` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | post_fit_same_data | Newtonian dynamic viscosity eta (Pa.s) and power-law consistency index K (Pa.s^n) of soluble-coffee extract vs temperature and water fraction. | The independently transcribed Telis-Romero 2001 Eq(10)/(13) viscosity closures reproduce the full digitized Table 1 (24 eta cells) and Table 2 (27 K cells) at about the authors' own reported fit error, cross-checking the digitization against the transcribed coefficients. |
| `sourcing2026.g10_liquor_rheology::gate_g10_viscosity_bulk_negligible` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | sign_or_compatibility | not_empirical | Darcy flow-suppression factor (mu_water / mu_local) implied by concentration-dependent liquor viscosity over a simulated shot's in-pore concentration field. | Driving cameron2020's own in-pore liquor concentration field through the MEASURED Telis-Romero 2001 eta(c,T) grid, the in-pore liquor never approaches saturation (peak mu < 1.15x water), so the constant-water-viscosity assumption used by every registered flow model gives <8% outlet flow suppression and >0.95 depth-averaged flow factor at espresso conditions. |
| `sourcing2026.g1_glassbead_analog::gate_g1_glassbead_closure_sane` | paper3 | resource_context | context_only | sign_or_compatibility | not_empirical | Parameter bounds and monotonicity of the cubic K_r(S)=((S-S_r)/(1-S_r))^3 curve. | The glass-bead analog closure loads with physically sane parameters (0<S_r<0.2, cubic n=3, alpha>0, K0>0) and yields a monotone-increasing relative permeability K_r(S) on [S_r,1]. |
| `sourcing2026.g3_pump_characteristic::gate_g3_pump_envelope_bounds_quadratic` | paper3 | resource_context | context_only | sign_or_compatibility | code_verification | Pump/basket pressure drop dP (bar) vs flow Q over the working range, bounded against manufacturer free-flow debit and deadhead endpoints. | The operative measured single-rig Waszkiewicz brewer quadratic (dP=aQ^2+bQ+c) stays inside the Ulka manufacturer envelope over the working flow band (dP below the 15-bar deadhead, free-flow > 1.90 g/s) — a bounds consistency check between two on-disk datasets. |
| `wadsworth2026.grindmap::gate_grindmap_polydispersity` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | code_verification | Polydispersivity S (moment ratio) vs grinder dial setting G. | Recomputing S=<R><R^2>/<R^3> from Table 1's printed moment columns reproduces the paper's printed S values (max err <5e-3), and the printed S rises with dial G within each coffee. |
| `wadsworth2026.grindmap::gate_grindmap_refit` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | Mean grain radius <R> vs dial G (linear grind map). | An OLS refit of <R>=beta*G+R0 to all 22 Table 1 samples is a high-R^2 (>0.99) monotone linear map whose predicted <R> at G=1 and G=11 bracket the measured range, reproducing the paper's corrected fit constants beta~5.805e-5, R0~1.380e-4. |
| `wadsworth2026.inertial::gate_inertial_darcy_recovery` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | code_verification | code_verification | Darcy-limit recovery of the Forchheimer momentum-equation root solver (code property). | As k_I->infinity the Forchheimer quadratic solver returns the Darcy velocity q=\|grad p\|k/mu to rtol 1e-6, and a finite k_I (Zhou closure) yields q below the Darcy value. |
| `wadsworth2026.inertial::gate_inertial_de1_audit` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | sign_or_compatibility | post_fit_same_data | Peak Forchheimer number Fo_F = rho*k*q/(mu*k_I) evaluated along the recorded trace. | Computing Fo_F along the real DE1 fixture-A tamped trace (Darcy k from the fitted kappa=1.196 via the kappa->SI chain, k_I from the ceramics-fitted Zhou closure) yields a peak Fo_F that is O(1), falls inside the backlog's 0.3-0.9 gusher-regime estimate, and exceeds the card's untamped espresso ceiling (0.0639) by >10x. |
| `wadsworth2026.inertial::gate_inertial_fo_band` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | not_empirical | Forchheimer number Fo_F band for the paper's worked espresso estimate. | Recomputing the card's worked espresso estimate (<R> 145-276 um, phi_p 0.3-0.5 via percolation k, q 5.36-5.74e-4 m/s, Zhou k_I) reproduces the paper's reported Forchheimer-number band Fo_F 0.0161-0.0639. |
| `wadsworth2026.inertial::gate_mo_reynolds_overlay` | paper3 | cited_science | context_only | source_curve_reproduction | code_verification | Pore-scale Reynolds-number band and apparent-k decline derived from mo2023 Fig 8a. | Computing Re=rho(k grad p/mu)d43/mu from mo2023's digitized Fig 8a at their SPH conditions reproduces mo's reported Re band 0.84-3.86 and shows the apparent Darcy k declining with grad p (Forchheimer). |
| `wadsworth2026.permeability::gate_wadsworth_collapse` | paper3 | cited_science | admissible | source_curve_reproduction | post_fit_same_data | Dimensionless collapse ratio k_measured / (k_star(phi_p,s_p)*phi_p^4.4) across the Table 1 grind rows, aggregated as a geometric mean. | The paper's percolation permeability form k = k_star(phi_p,s_p)*phi_p^4.4 collapses their own Table 1 measured permeabilities to a geometric-mean ratio near 0.9 (gate window 0.7 < GM < 1.2). |
| `waszkiewicz2025.poroelastic::gate_waszkiewicz_dynamic_9bar` | paper3 | method_demonstration | admissible | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | Mass flow Q(t) at 9 bar vs time (long-run mean g/s and Pearson correlation, t>=15 s). | With zero extra free parameters (static (P_c,Q_c) plus the dissolution sigmoid Phi(t)=m_d(t)/m0), Eq. 18 reproduces the measured 9-bar Q(t) ramp semi-quantitatively — long-run mean within 10% and correlation >0.90 over t>=15 s. |
| `waszkiewicz2025.poroelastic::gate_waszkiewicz_static_refit` | paper3 | cited_science | admissible | post_fit_reconstruction | post_fit_same_data | Steady-state basket-pressure vs mass-flow curve; recovered calibration parameters P_c, Q_c. | Refitting the poroelastic equilibrium closure (Eq. 16) to the 11-pressure long-run pressure-flow curve recovers the calibration pair (P_c,Q_c) ~ (12 bar, 1.90 g/s) and reproduces the paper's ingested static calibration to <0.05 bar / <0.01 g/s. |
